<!-- topic: tooling -->
<!-- sources: ADR-015, ADR-021, ADR-022, ADR-030 -->
<!-- compiled_by: gov-compile v0.6.0-rc4 -->
<!-- compiled_at: 2026-05-04T09:46:01Z -->

# Tooling

[edikt:directives:start]: #
- A shared module within the tier-2 boundary MAY be used, but MUST NOT cross into tier-1. (ref: ADR-015)
- A tool's tier MUST be declared in its command frontmatter (`tier: 1` or `tier: 2`). (ref: ADR-015)
- After uninstall, tier-1 command surface — files on disk, their content, their behavior — MUST be byte-equal to its pre-install state. (ref: ADR-015)
- Demoting a tool from tier-1 to tier-2 MUST require a major-version bump of edikt. (ref: ADR-015)
- Every tier-1 command file MUST be a `.md` or `.yaml` file; no tier-1 code is compiled; tier-1 install is file-copy only. (ref: ADR-015)
- INV-001 MUST apply to tier-1 without modification. (ref: ADR-015)
- INV-001 MUST apply verbatim to every tier-1 artifact with no exceptions. (ref: ADR-015)
- Parity between a tier-2 markdown command surface and its supporting code MUST be enforced by tests, not by code reuse. (ref: ADR-015)
- Promoting a tool from tier-2 to tier-1 MUST require a major-version bump of edikt. (ref: ADR-015)
- Tier-1 commands MUST NOT read from, write to, or depend on any tier-2 file or helper at runtime. (ref: ADR-015)
- Tier-1 files MUST NOT depend on tier-2 at runtime. (ref: ADR-015)
- Tier-2 tools MUST NEVER install pip helpers into the ambient system Python. (ref: ADR-015)
- Tier-2 tools MUST be installed via `edikt install <tool>`, never bundled in `install.sh`, never added to a user's environment without an explicit command. (ref: ADR-015)
- Tier-2 tools MUST declare `tier: 2` in their command file frontmatter. (ref: ADR-015)
- Tier-2 tools MUST fail fast with an actionable error message when prerequisites (Python version, venv creation, disk space) are unmet, before touching the filesystem. (ref: ADR-015)
- Tier-2 tools MUST pin exact versions (`==`) for their package dependencies in any `pyproject.toml` / equivalent manifest — no `>=` or floating ranges. (ref: ADR-015)
- Tier-2 tools MUST roll back partial installs: if install fails after some files were copied, those files are removed before exit. (ref: ADR-015)
- Tier-2 tools MUST use an isolated environment for any pip-installed helpers: dedicated venv under `~/.edikt/venv/<tool>/` or `pipx`. (ref: ADR-015)
- Tier-2 tools MUST verify their package-install artifacts against the release checksum manifest established by ADR-013 before installing. (ref: ADR-015)
- Uninstall MUST be idempotent: repeated runs exit 0 without error. (ref: ADR-015)
- `edikt uninstall <tool>` MUST remove tier-2 files and any package-managed dependencies for that tool. (ref: ADR-015)
- `install.sh` and the global installer MUST install only tier-1 artifacts. (ref: ADR-015)
- All future tier-2 helpers under `tools/<name>/` MUST be Go modules producing single static binaries. (ref: ADR-021)
- Each binary's SHA-256 MUST go into the existing `SHA256SUMS` (cosign-signed per ADR-016). (ref: ADR-021)
- Go dependency pinning MUST use `go.mod` + `go.sum` at build time. (ref: ADR-021)
- The bootstrap installer (`install.sh`) and the launcher (`bin/edikt`) MUST remain bash. (ref: ADR-021)
- The release workflow MUST cross-compile each helper for darwin-amd64, darwin-arm64, linux-amd64, linux-arm64. (ref: ADR-021)
- Tier-2 deterministic helpers MUST be implemented in Go. (ref: ADR-021)
- `bin/edikt install <helper>` MUST download the matching binary for the host platform, verify the signed SHA-256, chmod +x, and symlink into `$EDIKT_ROOT/tools/<name>`. (ref: ADR-021)
- For ALL other subcommands, the binary execs `edikt-shell` (the renamed bash launcher) with full stdin/stdout/stderr passthrough and `EDIKT_SHELL_CALLER=1` to prevent re-entry loops. (ref: ADR-022)
- No user-visible command (`edikt --help`, any subcommand) MUST NOT surface the word `edikt-shell` or any bash internals. (ref: ADR-022)
- The Go binary MUST be the user-facing `edikt` entry point. (ref: ADR-022)
- The `tools/edikt/.gitignore` MUST exclude compiled binary artifacts; the binary is shipped only via release assets (ADR-016), never committed to the repo. (ref: ADR-022)
- When `bin/edikt-shell` is absent, the Go binary MUST produce a clear error message (not a hang, not a panic). (ref: ADR-022)
- `install.sh` MUST place BOTH artifacts from the release tarball: `bin/edikt` (Go binary, user-facing) and `bin/edikt-shell` (renamed bash launcher, internal). (ref: ADR-022)
- CI MUST run tools/edikt/check/no-llm-in-tier-2.sh, which greps every non-test .go file under tools/edikt/ for exec.Command.*claude, exec.LookPath.*claude, and the literal string "claude", and fail on any unexempted match. (ref: ADR-030)
- Every tier-2 source file MUST be held to the same os/exec, net/http, no-LLM-import discipline as Phase B — the no-LLM purity gate applies project-wide, not just to phaseb. (ref: ADR-030)
- The locked extractor agent MUST remain a single templates/agents/sidecar-extractor.md artifact dispatched by tier-1 markdown via the host agent's subagent primitive, NEVER by the Go binary via exec.Command. (ref: ADR-030)
- Tier-2 Go binaries MUST NOT spawn, invoke, or shell out to any LLM CLI. (ref: ADR-030)
- When a tier-2 path needs an LLM round-trip, the tier-2 binary MUST: (1) perform mechanical work unaided, (2) write a partial sidecar with topic: needs-review for unrecoverable fields, (3) emit a machine-readable manifest of artifacts needing LLM resync on stderr or via --json, (4) exit cleanly. (ref: ADR-030)
[edikt:directives:end]: #
[edikt:directives:sha256]: # 91cf8da385fbf1c54adf7e8c73f3954a7c5762ce40489f98a7bc7bc27fd43051

[edikt:prohibitions:start]: #
## Prohibitions
- MUST NOT add a generic agent-dispatch abstraction inside Go that pluggably calls Claude, Codex, or other LLMs — every additional agent integration would require a Go release. (ref: ADR-030)
- MUST NOT defer tier-2 benchmark tooling to a separate downstream project — splitting it weakens the governance-holds-under-execution claim. (ref: ADR-015)
- MUST NOT implement tier-2 helpers in Python — requires user-side install friction (Python version, pipx, isolated venv) and produces non-static binaries that break on host dep changes. (ref: ADR-021)
- MUST NOT keep LLM dispatch in Go with silent skip on missing-binary failure — silent degradation to topic: needs-review without user-facing remediation is the bug this ADR was created to eliminate. (ref: ADR-030)
- MUST NOT keep the bash launcher and never migrate to Go — bash is untestable, not cross-platform (no Windows), and accumulates tech debt faster than Go. (ref: ADR-022)
- MUST NOT keep two separate user-facing binaries permanently — governance tools must have minimal surface area and two binaries causes user confusion. (ref: ADR-022)
- MUST NOT make the LLM CLI configurable via EDIKT_LLM_BIN — this papers over the architectural smell and leaves the Go binary owning dispatch, a poor fit for IDE-native agents with no CLI surface. (ref: ADR-030)
- MUST NOT perform a big-bang rewrite of all subcommands in Go at once — 3000 lines of bash handle many edge cases and a big-bang rewrite risks regressions for months. (ref: ADR-022)
- MUST NOT use Deno or Bun for tier-2 helpers — binaries bundle the full JS runtime (~60-100 MB), violating the release footprint target. (ref: ADR-021)
- MUST NOT weaken INV-001 to allow package dependencies in core commands — adding an exception at the invariant level erodes the core guarantee. (ref: ADR-015)
[edikt:prohibitions:end]: #
[edikt:prohibitions:sha256]: # b0bf95eb5bbf977479a49538dfa07e46c5a9c095da601c1552a5a6f1638308be
